actions.name: Switch to LED Bulbs
actions.short_description: Replace incandescent bulbs with ENERGY STAR-certified LED bulbs.
actions.detailed_description: Swap out older incandescent or CFL bulbs for high-efficiency LED bulbs in your most-used fixtures (kitchen, living room, exterior lights). Focus first on the 5–10 fixtures you use most. LEDs use up to 80% less energy and last much longer.

actions.name: Lower Thermostat by 2°F
actions.short_description: Reduce heating or cooling setpoint by two degrees.
actions.detailed_description: Adjust your thermostat two degrees lower in winter (or higher in summer) and stick with it for at least a month. Most households barely notice the difference in comfort, but energy savings can add up significantly over a year.

actions.name: Weekly Car-Free Day
actions.short_description: Commit to one day per week without driving.
actions.detailed_description: Choose one day each week where you avoid using a personal car altogether. Use walking, cycling, public transit, or carpooling instead. Start with local errands and commutes that are easiest to shift.

actions.name: Home Energy Audit (DIY)
actions.short_description: Conduct a basic do-it-yourself home energy audit.
actions.detailed_description: Use an online checklist or your utility’s guide to inspect drafts around windows and doors, check insulation, identify phantom power loads, and note old appliances. Create a list of top three fixes to address over the next three months.

actions.name: Smart Power Strips
actions.short_description: Install smart or switched power strips on electronics clusters.
actions.detailed_description: Identify areas with many electronics (TV, gaming console, computer setup) and plug them into smart or manually switched power strips. Turn them off fully when not in use to cut standby power draw.

actions.name: Meatless Mondays
actions.short_description: Go vegetarian at least one day a week.
actions.detailed_description: Plan and prepare plant-based meals every Monday (or another chosen day). Focus on simple substitutions like beans, lentils, and tofu in place of beef or lamb, which have the highest carbon footprints.

actions.name: Install Low-Flow Showerheads
actions.detailed_description: Replace at least one high-use showerhead with a low-flow model that meets WaterSense standards. This can cut water use and the energy needed to heat that water, often without noticeable change in comfort.

actions.name: Compost Kitchen Scraps
actions.short_description: Start a small compost bin for food scraps.
actions.detailed_description: Set up a countertop or under-sink compost pail for fruit and vegetable scraps, coffee grounds, and yard waste. Use a backyard pile, tumbler, or municipal green bin if available to divert organics from landfill.

actions.name: Eliminate Single-Use Bottles
actions.short_description: Carry a reusable bottle and avoid buying bottled water.
actions.detailed_description: Invest in a durable reusable water bottle and keep it with you when commuting, at work, or during errands. Refill from tap or filtered water instead of purchasing single-use plastic bottles.

actions.name: Upgrade to Efficient Appliances
actions.short_description: Plan and budget for ENERGY STAR appliance upgrades.
actions.detailed_description: When major appliances reach end-of-life, replace them with high-efficiency ENERGY STAR models. Start by listing approximate ages of your refrigerator, washer, and HVAC system and estimating replacement timing and costs.

actions.name: Native Pollinator Garden
actions.short_description: Plant a small garden with native, pollinator-friendly plants.
actions.detailed_description: Convert part of your yard, balcony, or community space into a native plant garden that supports bees, butterflies, and other pollinators. Choose species adapted to your local climate and avoid pesticides.

actions.name: Line Dry Laundry
actions.short_description: Air-dry at least half of your laundry loads.
actions.detailed_description: Use an indoor drying rack or outdoor clothesline for towels, shirts, and other easy-to-dry items. Aim to cut your dryer use by at least 50%, especially during warm months.

actions.name: Switch to Green Electricity Plan
actions.short_description: Enroll in a certified renewable electricity plan.
actions.detailed_description: If available, opt into a utility or third-party plan that sources most or all of your electricity from renewable sources like wind and solar. Review plan terms, certification, and potential cost differences before switching.

actions.name: Neighborhood Litter Walk
actions.short_description: Pick up litter during a weekly neighborhood walk.
actions.detailed_description: Bring gloves and a reusable bag on a weekly walk in your neighborhood, local park, or shoreline. Collect litter and dispose of it properly, focusing on plastics that might otherwise reach waterways.

articles.title: Plastic Pollution Basics: How Everyday Items Add Up
articles.subtitle: A beginner’s guide to understanding plastic waste
articles.content: Plastic is everywhere in modern life, from food packaging to clothing fibers. This introductory guide explains what plastic pollution is, where it comes from, and why it’s a problem for oceans, wildlife, and people. You’ll learn the difference between macroplastics and microplastics, how plastic travels through rivers to the sea, and what simple steps you can start taking today to reduce your own plastic footprint.
articles.summary: Learn what plastic pollution is, why it matters, and the main sources of plastic waste in daily life.

articles.title: 5 Easy Swaps to Cut Plastic at Home
articles.subtitle: Simple changes with big impact
articles.content: If you’re new to low-waste living, start with a few easy swaps. In this article we walk through five beginner-friendly changes: switching to a reusable water bottle, bringing your own shopping bags, avoiding plastic straws, choosing bar soap over bottled, and buying in bulk where possible. Each swap includes tips, estimated cost, and the impact on plastic waste.
articles.summary: Discover five simple plastic-reducing swaps you can make this week.

articles.title: Understanding Microplastics in Tap Water
articles.subtitle: What we know and how to respond
articles.content: Recent studies have found tiny plastic particles—microplastics—in many tap water samples. This article explains what microplastics are, how they enter water supplies, current research on health impacts, and practical steps you can take, from using basic filters to reducing plastic use at the source.
articles.summary: An overview of microplastics in drinking water for beginners.

articles.title: Plastic-Free Starter Kit: What You Really Need
articles.subtitle: Avoid the hype and focus on essentials
articles.content: You don’t need a suitcase full of fancy products to cut down on plastic. This starter guide focuses on a few essential items: a reusable bottle, a set of shopping and produce bags, a compact cutlery kit, and food storage containers you probably already own. We also cover low-cost alternatives and how to avoid over-buying new stuff in the name of sustainability.
articles.summary: A practical beginner checklist for starting your plastic-free journey.

articles.title: Where Does Your Plastic Go After the Bin?
articles.subtitle: Following the journey of household plastics
articles.content: Many people assume that placing plastic in a recycling bin guarantees it will be recycled. The reality is more complex. This article traces the typical journey of a plastic bottle, from curbside collection to sorting facilities, and explains why some plastics are recycled, some are burned, and others end up in landfills or overseas.
articles.summary: Track the path of household plastic waste from bin to final destination.

articles.title: Intro to Home Energy Efficiency
articles.subtitle: Save money and cut emissions at the same time
articles.content: Home energy efficiency is one of the fastest ways to lower your carbon footprint. This guide covers basic concepts like insulation, air sealing, and efficient appliances, along with a checklist you can use to spot quick wins in your own home.
articles.summary: A beginner overview of where homes waste energy and how to fix it.

articles.title: How Tree Planting Helps Cities Breathe
articles.subtitle: Urban forests and air quality explained
articles.content: Urban tree planting improves air quality, reduces heat, and supports wildlife. We explore case studies from several cities and practical ways residents can get involved in local tree-planting efforts.
articles.summary: Learn why urban tree planting is a powerful climate and health solution.

articles.title: Beginner’s Guide to Carbon Footprint Calculators
articles.subtitle: Understand what the numbers really mean
articles.content: Carbon footprint calculators estimate emissions from your home energy use, transportation, food, and waste. This article breaks down how they work, what data they need, and how to interpret the results without getting overwhelmed.
articles.summary: Learn how to use carbon calculators to set real-world goals.

articles.title: Ocean Conservation 101
articles.subtitle: Protecting blue planet ecosystems
articles.content: From coral reefs to polar seas, oceans regulate climate and support countless species. This primer explains major threats such as overfishing, warming, acidification, and pollution, and highlights the role of marine protected areas.
articles.summary: A broad introduction to ocean conservation challenges and solutions.

articles.title: Advanced Strategies for Zero-Waste Communities
articles.subtitle: From pilot projects to citywide systems
articles.content: Communities around the world are piloting ambitious zero-waste programs. We examine policy tools, funding models, and infrastructure changes that enable deep cuts in waste generation, with a focus on replicable strategies.
articles.summary: A detailed look at high-impact zero-waste policies for practitioners.

articles.title: Beginner’s Guide to Wildlife-Friendly Backyards
articles.subtitle: Support birds, pollinators, and small mammals at home
articles.content: Small changes in your yard or balcony—like planting native flowers, adding a birdbath, and reducing pesticide use—can create vital habitat. This guide walks you through assessing your space and choosing simple actions with big benefits for wildlife.
articles.summary: How to turn your outdoor space into a mini wildlife refuge.

articles.title: How to Read Conservation Project Transparency Scores
articles.subtitle: Make your donations count
articles.content: Transparency scores help donors assess how organizations report on finances, governance, and impact. This article decodes common rating systems and offers a checklist for choosing high-accountability conservation projects.
articles.summary: Learn to evaluate project transparency and impact before donating.

articles.title: Reducing Plastic at Work: Office-Friendly Tips
articles.subtitle: Small changes for teams and workplaces
articles.content: Workplaces generate large amounts of plastic waste, from coffee cups to packaging. This article suggests simple steps employees and managers can take: setting up refill stations, encouraging reusable dishware, and rethinking office supplies.
articles.summary: How to make your office more plastic-conscious.

articles.title: Designing Effective Conservation Challenges
articles.subtitle: Behavioral science for better engagement
articles.content: Conservation challenges can nudge people toward sustainable habits, but design matters. Drawing on behavioral science, we explore what makes challenges stick: clear prompts, timely feedback, social proof, and realistic difficulty levels.
articles.summary: Principles for designing conservation challenges that people actually complete.

articles.title: Intro to Sustainable Seafood
articles.subtitle: Choose ocean-friendly options
articles.content: Seafood can be both healthy and sustainable—if it’s sourced responsibly. Learn the basics of seafood guides, eco-labels, and what questions to ask restaurants and markets about where their fish comes from.
articles.summary: A beginner primer on sustainable seafood choices.

challenges.name: Unplug Standby Devices Each Night
challenges.description: Before bed, turn off a smart power strip or unplug devices like TVs and game consoles to avoid standby power use.

challenges.name: Shorter Showers
challenges.description: Keep your daily shower to 5 minutes or less to save hot water and energy.

challenges.name: Lights Out in Empty Rooms
challenges.description: Do a quick sweep of your home each evening to switch off unnecessary lights.

challenges.name: Weekly Thermostat Check-In
challenges.description: Once a week, confirm your thermostat schedule matches your real routine and adjust by 1–2°F for efficiency.

challenges.name: Pack a Reusable Kit
challenges.description: Carry a reusable bottle, mug, and bag whenever you leave the house to avoid single-use items.

challenges.name: No Single-Use Plastics Today
challenges.description: Go through one full day without accepting any single-use plastic bags, cutlery, or bottles.

challenges.name: Car-Free Commute Day
challenges.description: Once a week, use walking, biking, or public transit for your main commute or errand trip.

challenges.name: Combine Errands into One Trip
challenges.description: Plan your errands to reduce the number of separate car trips each week.

challenges.name: Meatless Meal of the Day
challenges.description: Choose at least one meat-free meal each day, focusing on beans, lentils, or seasonal vegetables.

challenges.name: Water-Wise Dishwashing
challenges.description: Only run your dishwasher when full, or if washing by hand, fill a basin instead of letting water run.

challenges.name: Share One Conservation Fact
challenges.description: Once a week, share a conservation tip or fact with a friend, family member, or on social media.

challenges.name: Weekly Waste Audit Snapshot
challenges.description: Pick one day each week to quickly review what you’re throwing away and spot one item you could reduce or replace.

conservation_projects.name: Urban Wildlife Corridors Fund
conservation_projects.short_description: Support the creation of safe passages for city wildlife.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: This project works with city planners and communities to design and build wildlife corridors—greenways, overpasses, and underpasses—that allow animals to move safely between fragmented habitats in urban areas. Funds support mapping, community engagement, and on-the-ground construction.

conservation_projects.name: Coastal Sea Turtle Nesting Protection
conservation_projects.short_description: Help protect sea turtle nests on critical beaches.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: Local teams and volunteers patrol nesting beaches, relocate at-risk nests, and work with communities to reduce light pollution and beach traffic during nesting season. Donations fund equipment, training, and community outreach.

conservation_projects.name: Rainforest Protection Alliance
conservation_projects.short_description: Safeguard primary tropical forests from deforestation.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: The alliance partners with Indigenous communities and local governments to secure legal protections for intact rainforest areas, support sustainable livelihoods, and monitor deforestation via satellite.

conservation_projects.name: Blue Oceans Cleanup Initiative
conservation_projects.short_description: Scale up plastic cleanup and prevention in key rivers.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: This initiative deploys river barriers and nets to intercept plastic before it reaches the ocean, while also supporting local waste sorting and recycling efforts. Donor funds help maintain cleanup infrastructure and fund education campaigns.

conservation_projects.name: Community Tree-Planting Partnerships
conservation_projects.short_description: Plant climate-smart trees in underserved neighborhoods.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: Working with local groups, this project plants shade trees along streets and near schools in communities with limited tree cover. It includes long-term maintenance plans and job training for local residents.

conservation_projects.name: Household Solar for Low-Income Families
conservation_projects.short_description: Bring rooftop solar to households that need savings most.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: This climate project helps install rooftop solar panels for low-income households through grants and zero-interest financing. It reduces energy bills and emissions while building local clean energy jobs.

conservation_projects.name: Plastic-Free Coastlines Campaign
conservation_projects.short_description: Cut plastic waste along priority coastlines.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: This campaign supports local partners to install refill stations, promote reusable containers, and enforce bans on key single-use plastics near beaches and ports.

conservation_projects.name: Marine Protected Areas Accelerator
conservation_projects.short_description: Strengthen enforcement in existing marine reserves.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: Many marine protected areas exist only on paper. This project funds patrols, monitoring, and community co-management to ensure marine reserves deliver real conservation outcomes.

conservation_projects.name: Urban Pollinator Pathways
conservation_projects.short_description: Create connected networks of pollinator habitat in cities.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: By working with schools, businesses, and residents, this project plants native flowers and removes pesticide use along key routes, enabling pollinators to move safely through urban landscapes.

conservation_projects.name: Climate Education for Schools
conservation_projects.short_description: Equip teachers with reliable climate curriculum.
conservation_projects.detailed_description: This general conservation project develops open-source climate and conservation lesson plans, trains teachers, and supports student-led sustainability projects.

quiz_questions.text: How is your home primarily heated in winter?
On an average month, how high is your electricity bill?
How often do you adjust your thermostat when you leave home for several hours?
In a typical week, how many miles do you drive in personal vehicles?
How often do you use public transit, walking, or cycling instead of driving?
Which of the following waste reduction habits do you already practice regularly?
How often do you buy drinks in single-use plastic bottles?
On average, how many meals per week include red meat (beef or lamb)?
How often do you end up throwing away uneaten leftovers or spoiled food?
Do you have any water-efficient fixtures installed (e.g., low-flow showerheads, faucet aerators)?
How long are your typical showers?
Which areas are you most interested in improving first?
How much time per week can you realistically commit to new conservation habits?
Do you regularly turn off lights and electronics when they’re not in use?
How often do you combine errands into a single trip instead of multiple short trips?

volunteer_groups.name: Midtown Atlanta Urban Wildlife Volunteers
volunteer_groups.description: A local group focused on monitoring and improving habitat for birds, pollinators, and small mammals in Midtown parks and neighborhoods.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Meets every Saturday morning and one weekday evening each month in Midtown Atlanta.

volunteer_groups.name: Piedmont Park Wildlife Habitat Stewards
volunteer_groups.description: Volunteers restore native plants, maintain bird boxes, and monitor wildlife in and around Piedmont Park.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Bi-weekly workdays on Saturday mornings; occasional evening workshops.

volunteer_groups.name: Atlanta BeltLine Wildlife Watch
volunteer_groups.description: Citizen science group documenting urban wildlife along the BeltLine trail and advocating for habitat-friendly design.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Monthly evening walks and quarterly weekend surveys.

volunteer_groups.name: Chattahoochee River Clean & Kayak Crew
volunteer_groups.description: Volunteers paddle sections of the Chattahoochee River to collect trash and monitor water quality.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Monthly weekend paddling cleanups; seasonal weekday events.

volunteer_groups.name: Atlanta Climate Action Network
volunteer_groups.description: A regional network organizing climate policy advocacy, educational events, and local campaigns.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Monthly planning meetings plus campaign-specific events.

volunteer_groups.name: North Atlanta Forest Friends
volunteer_groups.description: Group focused on invasive species removal and native tree planting in nearby forested parks.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Two Saturday mornings per month, rotating park locations north of Midtown.

volunteer_groups.name: Coastal Shorebird Guardians
volunteer_groups.description: Volunteers monitor nesting shorebirds and educate beachgoers about wildlife-friendly behavior.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Seasonal weekend shifts on coastal beaches; virtual trainings in spring.

volunteer_groups.name: Midtown Green Neighbors
volunteer_groups.description: A general conservation group for residents interested in gardening, tree planting, and neighborhood cleanups.
volunteer_groups.meeting_schedule_description: Monthly potluck meetings and ad-hoc weekend projects.

quiz_options.text: Wood, oil, or other fossil fuels
I regularly bring my own reusable bags and bottles
I compost food scraps at home or via a service
I avoid products with excessive packaging
Rarely (a few times per month or less)
0–2 meals with red meat per week
3–5 meals with red meat per week
6 or more meals with red meat per week

events.title: Mission District Street Tree Planting Day
events.subtitle: Greening the blocks around 24th Street
events.description: Join neighbors and local nonprofits to plant drought-tolerant street trees in San Francisco's Mission District. Training, tools, and light snacks provided.
events.requirements: Wear closed-toe shoes and clothes you don't mind getting dirty. All ages welcome; youth under 16 must be accompanied by an adult.

events.title: Bernal Heights Sunday Tree Planting
events.subtitle: Shade for neighborhood sidewalks
events.description: Help expand the tree canopy in Bernal Heights by planting and staking young street trees. No experience needed; staff will provide training.
events.requirements: Gloves and tools provided. Bring a reusable water bottle.

events.title: Bayview Community Tree Planting
events.subtitle: Planting climate-smart trees in Bayview
events.description: Partner with local youth groups to plant shade trees along key streets in Bayview, improving air quality and urban habitat.
events.requirements: Spanish and English language support available. Please arrive 15 minutes early for check-in.

events.title: Oakland Waterfront Tree Planting
events.subtitle: Cooling the shoreline trail
events.description: Join volunteers along the Oakland waterfront to plant native trees and shrubs that will provide shade and habitat along the Bay Trail.
events.requirements: All tools provided. Event will proceed light rain or shine.

events.title: San Mateo Creek Tree Planting
events.subtitle: Restoring a local watershed
events.description: Help restore native trees and shrubs along San Mateo Creek. This family-friendly event includes a short walk-and-talk on creek ecology.
events.requirements: Recommended for ages 8 and up. Bring sun protection and a packed lunch.

events.title: Napa County Hillside Reforestation Day
events.subtitle: Planting climate-resilient trees after wildfire
events.description: Spend the day planting native tree species on a hillside property recovering from recent wildfires. Transportation options from the Bay Area are available.
events.requirements: Sturdy hiking shoes required. Carpool encouraged; bus from San Francisco available with separate registration.

events.title: Weekday Tree Planting & Maintenance
events.subtitle: Pruning, mulching, and a few new trees
events.description: A smaller weekday crew will help water recently planted street trees, add mulch, and plant a few additional saplings in the Mission and Potrero neighborhoods.
events.requirements: Weekday volunteers only; ideal for flexible schedules and local businesses.

events.title: Evening Ocean Conservation Webinar: Saving Our Reefs
events.subtitle: Community actions to protect coral ecosystems
events.description: Learn how climate change, pollution, and overfishing are impacting coral reefs, and discover practical ways individuals and communities can help. Includes Q&A with marine scientists.
events.requirements: Registration required to receive the Zoom link. Recommended for ages 12 and up.

events.title: Lunchtime Ocean Conservation Q&A
events.subtitle: Ask a marine biologist
events.description: A casual lunchtime session where you can ask a marine biologist about ocean health, marine protected areas, and what actions matter most.
events.requirements: Internet connection required; questions can be submitted in advance.

events.title: Ocean Plastics & Marine Wildlife
events.subtitle: How plastic pollution affects ocean life
events.description: This interactive online event explores the journey of plastic from land to sea and its impacts on marine wildlife. Includes breakout discussions on personal and policy solutions.
events.requirements: Headphones and a microphone recommended for breakout discussions.

events.title: Global Oceans Summit Preview Webinar
events.subtitle: What to expect from this year’s summit
events.description: A preview of the upcoming Global Oceans Summit, highlighting key themes such as blue carbon, deep-sea mining, and coastal resilience.
events.requirements: Registration required; recording will be sent to all registrants.

events.title: Plastic Pollution Basics: Live Webinar
events.subtitle: A beginner-friendly introduction
events.description: Following our popular article series, this webinar walks through plastic pollution basics and everyday ways to reduce plastic use at home and work.
events.requirements: Ideal for beginners. Live captions available.

events.title: Urban Wildlife Habitat Walk
events.subtitle: Spotting city wildlife and their habitats
events.description: Take a guided walk through a city park to learn how birds, pollinators, and small mammals use urban green spaces, and how you can improve habitat at home.
events.requirements: Wear comfortable walking shoes; binoculars optional.

events.title: Earth Day Climate Action Fair
events.subtitle: Local solutions, global impact
events.description: Celebrate Earth Day with local organizations showcasing climate solutions, from home energy upgrades to low-carbon transportation. Includes short talks, booths, and kid-friendly activities.
events.requirements: Open to the public; no registration required.

events.title: Getting Started with Your Conservation Dashboard
events.subtitle: Tour the tools: action planner, calculator, and challenges
events.description: A live online orientation to the website's tools, including the action planner, carbon footprint calculator, and lifestyle challenges. Learn how to set goals and track your progress.
events.requirements: Account on the website recommended but not required.

events.title: Neighborhood Plastic Cleanup Walk
events.subtitle: Collecting litter before it reaches the ocean
events.description: Join neighbors for a two-hour litter walk focusing on plastic packaging, bottles, and wrappers. We’ll track what we collect and discuss ways to prevent this waste in the first place.
events.requirements: Gloves and bags provided. Children welcome with adult supervision.
